title: alsine graminifolia subsp. clandestina
intro: Alsine graminifolia subsp. clandestina, also known as creeping saxifrage, is a sub-species of the Alsine graminifolia plant, which is a perennial herb native to certain regions. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of creeping saxifrage include wound healing, treatment of skin conditions, and relief from pain and inflammation. Alsine graminifolia subsp. clandestina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and is also sometimes used in folk remedies for its perceived benefits.
